title: gentianella campestris subsp. campestris
intro: Gentianella campestris subsp. campestris, also known as alpine gentian, is a species of flowering plant in the gentian family that is native to Europe and parts of Asia. It is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its bitter compounds that have been traditionally used to stimulate digestion and relieve symptoms of indigestion. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, acting as an antioxidant, and potentially lowering blood sugar levels. Gentianella campestris subsp. campestris can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are often used to aid in the treatment of digestive issues and other health concerns.
